title: vivo is deciding between three 200MP sensors for the X500 Ultra's telephoto
description: The vivo X500 Ultra isn't expected any time soon, and it will likely arrive on the global stage in early 2027, but that doesn't stop the rumor mill from spinning. The latest leak comes from the renowned Weibo tipster Digital Chat Station, and it's all about the telephoto camera. According to the report, vivo's imaging team still hasn't decided on the right 200MP sensor for the periscope telephoto camera. Engineers are split between three sensor sizes - 1/1.12", 1/1.3" and 1/1.4". Of course, the larger sensor means better low-light performance and generally better image quality, but it...

title: Lava Virat V1 and Virat V1 5G's specs revealed, launch rescheduled
description: Lava announced a partnership with Flipkart last week for its new Virat smartphone series and said the Virat V1 would launch in India on July 21. Well, the launch has been rescheduled to July 24, and Lava has announced it will unveil the Virat V1 and Virat V1 5G as the first smartphones in the Virat series next Friday. Additionally, Lava and Flipkart revealed the specs of both Virat series smartphones. The Lava Virat V1 5G will be powered by the Unisoc T8200 SoC, have 4GB RAM and 64GB of onboard storage, and run Android 16 out of the box without ads or bloatware. It will feature a 6.75"...

title: The new 12.2-inch Asus Pad is on its way to India, here's when it lands
description: Last month, Asus re-entered the Android tablet market by unveiling the 12.2-inch Asus Pad. Today, the company has confirmed that the device is headed to India, where it will launch on August 6 at 12:00 noon local time. Unfortunately, the price hasn't been revealed yet. The Asus Pad's screen uses OLED technology and has a resolution of 2800x1840, a 144Hz refresh rate, a 3:2 aspect ratio, and 2,000-nit peak brightness. The tablet has four speakers which support Dolby Atmos 360, and it's powered by the MediaTek Dimensity 8300 SoC, paired with 8GB of LPDDR5X RAM and 128GB or 256GB of...

title: RedMagic Astra 2 review
RedMagic Astra 2 gaming tablet goes global
description: The RedMagic Astra 2 debuted in late June in China under the "Gaming Tablet 5 Pro" moniker, and now, users outside of China can get one, too. There are no obvious changes to the design or hardware, so global markets get what the Chinese market does. The tablet is built around a 9.06-inch LTPO OLED screen with a 185Hz refresh rate and extra-fast touch response time, with an average touch sampling rate of 300Hz. The Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 5 chipset sits behind the wheel, and you can get the tablet with either 12GB/256GB or 16GB/512GB memory. The main chipset is aided by the dedicated...

title: Samsung's mobile division might see its first quarterly loss ever
description: While Samsung Electronics expects record-breaking profits for Q2 2026, mainly thanks to its semiconductor business, the mobile division might see its first-ever quarterly loss. According to DealSite South Korea analysis, Samsung MX's earnings could range between KRW 1.9 trillion ($1.38 billion) profit and KRW 1.5 trillion ($1.09 billion) loss. Still, the most probable scenario is a loss of $364-$729 million. This might be surprising for some given the strong Galaxy S26 series performance, but if you've been following the memory chip prices lately, that probably doesn't come as a...

title: Nothing is one of India's fastest-growing smartphone brands, Counterpoint Research reveals
description: According to a new report from Counterpoint Research (CR), the Indian smartphone market declined 10% in the second quarter of this year (April-June) compared to the same period last year. This was the biggest decline in shipments in the country in the past six years. Chinese brands had their lowest share of the overall market for a second quarter since 2020. vivo remained the leader of the market but only because CR doesn't add all sub-brands' market share to the main brand's. Individually vivo has a 17.8% share, but when you count iQOO too that gets it to 19.5%. However, while Oppo seems...

title: There will be a vanilla Poco X8 after all, HyperOS source code suggests
description: The Poco X8 Pro and X8 Pro Max were both announced in March, but after months had passed, many believe that the vanilla Poco X8 might have been canceled. No one had an explanation, but we all just assumed that Xiaomi scrapped the vanilla Poco X8. Until now, that is. Poco X8 Pro Max In Xiaomi's HyperOS source code, X user Kacper Skryzpek unearthed solid evidence that the Poco X8 exists and it has a 50MP main camera and 2MP depth sensor. And if that sounds familiar, it's likely because the Poco X8 will actually be a rebranded Chinese Redmi Note 17 Pro 5G. The latter launched recently...

title: Tecno Camon 50 Ultra launched in India
description: As promised, Tecno today launched the Camon 50 Ultra in India, which will go on sale starting July 21 exclusively through Amazon.in. The Tecno Camon 50 Ultra is priced at INR39,999 and comes in three colors in India - Nebula Titanium, Cypress Green, and Misty Purple. However, for reasons best known to Tecno, the Camon 50 Ultra's RAM amount and storage space in India haven't been revealed. That said, the specs and features of Tecno Camon 50 Ultra in India are the same as other markets, meaning you get the Dimensity 7400 Ultimate SoC, a 6.78" 144Hz curved AMOLED display of...

title: iQOO confirms Z11 Lite's price segment while revealing more details about it
description: iQOO will launch the Z11 Lite on July 24, and ahead of the launch, the brand has confirmed that the smartphone will be priced under INR20,000 in India. Additionally, iQOO revealed that the Z11 Lite's notched display will have a 120Hz refresh rate and 1,200 nits peak brightness, while the primary camera on the rear will use a 50MP Sony sensor. The Z11 Lite will have up to 6GB RAM and 256GB of storage onboard. iQOO previously confirmed that the Z11 Lite will run OriginOS 6 out of the box and receive security updates for four years. The brand has now revealed that the smartphone will...

title: Oppo K15 to launch next week with an 8,000mAh battery, in-built cooling fan
description: After launching the K15 Pro lineup in China back in April, Oppo is now preparing to introduce the standard K15. The smartphone has been listed on Oppo's official online store, revealing its design, key specifications, and color options. The Oppo K15 is set to launch in China on July 24. Like the K15 Pro and K15 Pro+, it will feature a built-in cooling fan. The smartphone will be powered by the MediaTek Dimensity 7360 Super chipset and include a 4,950mm² vapor chamber. It will sport a 6.59-inch AMOLED display and pack an 8,000mAh battery with support for 80W wired fast...

title: Leaked Huawei Mate XT 2 patent hints at a new design and folding mechanism
description: Back in April, a rumor claimed that Huawei would unveil the Mate XT2 in October. Now, a newly surfaced patent for the alleged tri-fold smartphone hints at a new design and folding mechanism. The patent images, shared by well-known tipster Ice Universe on X, suggest that the Mate XT2 could adopt an inward-folding design, replacing the Z-shaped folding mechanism used on the Mate XT and Mate XTs. The folding mechanism appears to be similar to the Samsung Galaxy Z Trifold, although it works differently. Based on the patent images, the Mate XT2 will offer a cover display alongside a...

title: Hasselblad's Phocus Mobile finally lands on Android
description: 16 years after the first version became available on iOS and 6 years after Phocus Mobile 2 was released for iPhones, Hasselblad's Phocus Mobile is now finally available on Android too. It works with the Hasselblad X2D II, X2D 100C, and 907X & CFV 100C cameras. The app delivers the end-to-end Hasselblad Natural Color Solution (HNCS) experience on mobile, combining AI-powered noise reduction with a comprehensive editing suite and photo management tools. In the Android app, you can now review, edit, organize, and export images. The colors, tonal gradations, and highlight details are...

title: Samsung is adding yet another preinstalled app to Galaxy phones, but there's a silver lining
description: Companies can’t help themselves and do things that might have sounded nice in the boardroom but are met with groans from actual consumers. We suspect this will be one of those things – Samsung US just announced that select Galaxy phones and tablets will come with the Amazon Music app pre-installed. To be fair, the bloatware comes with a perk – you get a free 3-month subscription. This gives you access to all the songs and podcasts on the service as well as one free audiobook from Audible per month. The Amazon Music Unlimited subscription is $13 a month and you can only get a 1-month...

title: Google rebrands NotebookLM as Gemini Notebook
description: Google's NotebookLM started off as a small Google Labs experiment back in 2023, but it has grown tremendously since then, reaching more than 30 million people and over 600,000 organizations. Thus, the company has realized that the product has basically outgrown its name. So, today, Google is announcing that it is renaming NotebookLM to Gemini Notebook. It will (at least for now) remain a standalone product "focused on being your premier research tool", but it will also "do more across the Google ecosystem, including inside the Gemini app and Google Search", the company says. ...

title: New leak reveals Apple's plans for the OLED iPad mini and the next base iPad
description: According to a new report from Bloomberg's Mark Gurman, Apple is going to launch the OLED iPad mini "as early as this fall". It will be released by October, apparently. A recent rumor claimed that while the iPad mini will have an OLED display, it will not get ProMotion so it will be stuck with a 60Hz refresh rate. The OLED iPad mini may be more expensive than the current model, which now starts at $599 following Apple's recent price hike. This is the current iPad mini Apple is reportedly also working on a new iPad Air with an OLED screen, while the base iPad will continue to use LCD...

title: EU adds exemptions to its removable battery rule for smartwatches, fitness trackers, ear buds
description: Back in 2022, the EU prepared new legislation that will bring back user-replaceable batteries on electronic devices. This will go into effect in February 2027, but some companies are already making the jump – for example, Nintendo released a special Switch 2 with a replaceable battery. But what about devices where having a replaceable battery might affect features such as water resistance? Well, the EU just added several exemptions just for such cases. An important category is the so-called “wet appliances”. These include: “wearable devices, such as wireless earbuds, heart rate monitor...

title: Infinix Xpad 30 Pro surfaces in retailer listing, here are its main specs
description: Infinix just unveiled the Hot 70 Pro today, and the company is also working on a new tablet. Meet the Infinix Xpad 30 Pro, which has been prematurely listed on a Belarusian retailer’s website. A listing for the device was also spotted on Infinix's official website too for a very short while before it was taken down. Judging by the name, this seems pretty obviously meant to be the successor to the Xpad 20 Pro, which was released last September. The Xpad 30 Pro is powered by MediaTek’s Helio G200 Ultimate chipset, and it comes with an 11-inch display with Eye-Care technology and "2.5K"...

title: European Commission forces Google to open Android to third-party AI assistants, share search data
description: Today the European Commission (EC) has announced two legally binding Digital Markets Act (DMA) decisions it's made forcing Google to open up Android and Search to competitors. First, Google has to give third-party AI assistants on Android access to key system features that are currently only available to Google's own Gemini. Thus, when someone sets a third-party AI chatbot as their default assistant, it will be able to have a similar feature set. Right now 60% of EU users who have an Android device find third-party AI assistants less attractive than Gemini specifically because they can't...

title: Samsung Galaxy Tab S12 Ultra appears in a new set of renders, specs confirmed
description: While the upcoming Galaxy Unpacked event gets all the attention lately, which is understandable given that Samsung is expected to announce not two but three foldables, the rumor mill never stops spinning, so we get to see one of the company's upcoming flagship tablets in a new set of renders. The notorious OnLeaks tipster partnered with Smartphone Checker to create CAD-based renders of the upcoming Galaxy Tab S12 Ultra tablet. Of course, the renders are based on insider information about the flagship tablet. The renders show an essentially untouched design and dimensions compared to...
